name: Edward G. Robinson
abstract: Edward Goldenberg Robinson (born Emanuel Goldenberg; December 12, 1893 – January 26, 1973) was an American actor who was popular during Hollywood's Golden Age. After making his stage debut in 1913, he rose to stardom with his performance as the title character in Little Caesar (1931) and became well known for his portrayals of gangsters.

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: Edward G. Robinson, you know. When they -- Alexander Graham Bell, Spencer Tracy portrayed the life.

about_quotes.speaker: Clark Terry
about_quotes.evidence: we were in the Strand Theater with, in those days, the picture got the top billing. “Key Largo” with Edward G. Robinson, Humphrey Bogart and Count Basie and Billie Holiday.
about_quotes.doc_title: Clark Terry interviewed by Joe Williams and Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, May 19, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: One of my little friend’s father was Harold Lloyd. He had an estate in L.A. that was unbelievable. And Edward G. Robinson’s son, and Wallace Berry’s daughter, etc., etc.
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Holmes
about_quotes.evidence: So I went to Edward G. Robinson in his dressing room, and I asked him for a photograph. And he gave it to me.

about_quotes.speaker: Alberta Hunter
about_quotes.evidence: Edward G. Robinson brought Mr. Bate, Fred Bate in, and Mr. Bate heard me sing

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
